title: Russian Blini Fermentation Casserole
description: A comforting casserole that combines strong white flour with an intense fermentation process, all topped with a flambe finish. This recipe is free from dairy products for those with dietary restrictions.

ingredients.name: Cultured milk alternative
ingredients.notes: such as soy milk or almond milk

instructions: Heat the water to 90°F in a large mixing bowl.
Add the cultured milk alternative, yeast, and salt. Stir to combine.
Gradually add in the flour to make a sticky dough.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Cover the bowl with a clean towel and let the dough rest in a warm place for 4 hours.
Once rested, roll out the dough to a thickness of 1/4 inch.
Place the dough in a greased 9 x 13 inch baking dish.
Dimple the dough with a spoon and let it rest for another 2 hours.
Bake the casserole for 25 minutes or until the crust is golden brown.
While the casserole is baking, melt the vegan butter in a pan over medium heat.
Add the paprika and stir for 1 minute.
Remove the casserole from the oven and brush the melted butter mixture evenly over the top.
Return the casserole to the oven and bake for an additional 5 minutes, or until the top is caramelized.
Remove the casserole from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving.
